title: Widget load failures silently fall back to empty state
body:
## Problem
When Yahoo Finance API returns 0 results for a symbol (e.g., invalid ticker, delisted stock), widgets display nothing without user feedback. The MarketIndexCards, MacroIndicators, and CryptoTracker components render null/dash characters for missing quotes, but there's no skeleton loader, loading state, or error message to distinguish between "still loading" and "data unavailable."

## Evidence
- MarketIndexCards.tsx:49-57: Quote data checked via `quotes[i]?.data` but no loading/error state shown
- CryptoTracker.tsx:25-26: `results[i]?.isLoading` exists but never renders in UI
- MacroIndicators.tsx:36-66: No fallback when indicator data missing
- WatchlistHeatmap.tsx:16-87: Returns null silently when watchlist empty

## Expected
Widgets should:
1. Show skeleton/shimmer while loading
2. Distinguish "no data" from "loading"
3. Show retry button for failed requests

## Proposed fix
- Add `isLoading` state visualization for all quote-dependent widgets
- Create shared `<QuoteLoadingState>` component
- Use react-query `isError` flag to render error boundary

## Impact
Users can't tell if widgets are broken or just slow, degrading dashboard UX during API outages.

title: Search debounce timing (180ms) misses keystroke rhythm
body:
## Problem
The SearchBar debounces at 180ms (SearchBar.tsx:15), which is slower than typical user keystroke intervals (70-120ms). This causes intermediate search states where the user has typed 3-4 characters but results haven't arrived yet, creating perceived lag. Additionally, when results do arrive via `useSearch(debounced)`, there's no visual indication of what triggered the query.

## Evidence
- SearchBar.tsx:14-17: `setTimeout(..., 180)` debounce
- SearchBar.tsx:27: Results rendered immediately after debounce fires without loading state
- SearchBar.tsx:53-55: Only `Enter` and `Escape` keys handled; no arrow navigation for accessibility

## Expected
1. Debounce tuned to 70-100ms for real-time feel
2. Loading spinner shown while `useSearch` fetches
3. Keyboard navigation (up/down arrows) for result selection

## Proposed fix
- Reduce debounce to 100ms
- Add `useSearch` loading indicator
- Implement arrow-key focused result selection
- Cache recent searches to reduce API calls

## Impact
Searchability feels sluggish; power users can't navigate results without mouse. Increased API load from repeated searches.

title: Sector heatmap color math breaks with 0% change
body:
## Problem
The SectorHeatmap color interpolation (SectorHeatmap.tsx:20-32) uses `color-mix()` CSS function that doesn't guarantee proper rendering across browsers. Additionally, the clamping to [-3, 3]% range means a sector with exactly 0% change still gets `bg-[var(--color-bg-elev)]`, but adjacent cells with ±0.1% get different colors. This violates Heatmap UX principle that similar values should have similar colors.

## Evidence
- SectorHeatmap.tsx:22-25: `Math.max(-3, Math.min(3, pct))` clamps without smoothing
- SectorHeatmap.tsx:25: `color-mix(in_srgb,...)` not supported in Safari <16
- SectorHeatmap.tsx:49: 3-6 column grid doesn't reflow well on narrow mobile

## Expected
1. Smooth color gradient without hard clipping
2. Cross-browser compatible color generation
3. Responsive grid that handles mobile widths

## Proposed fix
- Replace `color-mix()` with computed RGB values
- Use sigmoid function instead of hard clamp for smooth gradation
- Add `sm:grid-cols-2` breakpoint for mobile

## Impact
Sector performance misrepresented on some devices; visual inconsistency breaks trend analysis.

title: react-grid-layout layout reset doesn't persist state
body:
## Problem
The FinanceHome reset button calls `reset()` (FinanceHome.tsx:73) which resets in-memory state but doesn't immediately save. Users can reset, then accidentally close the app without clicking Save, losing the action. Additionally, the dirty flag logic doesn't distinguish between manual reset vs. dragging, so a drag-only session shows "Save layout" even though nothing changed.

## Evidence
- FinanceHome.tsx:72-79: Reset button manually sets `dirty = true` but doesn't auto-save
- FinanceHome.tsx:49-89: `onChange` fires on every drag, but `dirty` tracks layout only
- useLayout.ts:43-67: No localStorage fallback if DB is unavailable
- FinanceHome.tsx:51: All breakpoints (lg/md/sm/xs) use same layout—no mobile-specific optimization

## Expected
1. Reset should save immediately or prompt confirmation
2. Dirty flag only true when layout actually changed
3. localStorage cache for offline resilience
4. Separate mobile layout preset

## Proposed fix
- Auto-save on reset with toast confirmation
- Compute layout hash to detect real changes vs. no-op drags
- Add localStorage backup in useLayout
- Create `mobile` layout config with 4-column grid

## Impact
Users lose layout customizations unexpectedly; no mobile optimization creates unusable dashboard on tablets.

title: Market indices data staleness: staleTime tuned for US hours only
body:
## Problem
MarketIndexCards, MacroIndicators, and related widgets use hardcoded `staleTime` settings (e.g., SectorRotation.tsx:56: `30 * 60_000`, WatchlistEntrySignals.tsx:31: `30 * 60_000`) that assume US market hours. When markets close at 4 PM ET, these widgets continue serving stale data until next trading day. No logic accounts for DST transitions or pre-market hours.

## Evidence
- SectorRotation.tsx:56: `staleTime: 30 * 60_000` (30 min) doesn't respect market close
- WatchlistEntrySignals.tsx:31-46: Historical queries stale for 30 min even on weekends
- MacroCalendar.tsx:15: No DST handling when computing event dates
- YieldCurve.tsx:19-22: Tries to parse ^TNX both as bps and as percent without validation

## Expected
1. Dynamic staleTime based on market hours (US, EU, Asia regions)
2. Invalidate cache at 4 PM ET on trading days
3. DST-aware date calculations
4. Separate staleTime for pre-market (higher) vs. intraday (lower)

## Proposed fix
- Create `useMarketHours()` hook returning { isOpen, nextOpen, timezone }
- Set staleTime = isOpen ? 5*60_000 : 60*60_000
- Use `Intl.DateTimeFormat` with 'America/New_York' for DST
- Validate yield data format before rendering

## Impact
Post-market traders see stale S&P data; weekends show 3-day-old macro numbers.

title: Number formatting inconsistency: negative sign placement varies
body: ## Problem Price and percentage formatting across widgets places negative signs inconsistently. TopMovers.tsx shows `-2.5%` in some places and `signColor(pct)` in others. MarketIndexCards.tsx:75 uses `(q.change > 0 ? '+' : '') + q.change.toFixed(2)` but SectorHeatmap uses `fmtPct()` without explicit sign. This violates accessibility (screen readers read `-` before number in some cases), makes comparison harder, and looks buggy. ## Evidence - TopMovers.tsx:61,84: Uses `signColor()` helper but formatting varies - MarketIndexCards.tsx:70-75: Manual sign logic vs. fmtPrice/fmtPct helpers - WatchlistHeatmap.tsx:73-76: `fmtPct()` without consistent prefix - MacroIndicators.tsx:53-60: Yield shows `4.35%` but oil shows `$85.20`, inconsistent units ## Expected 1. Unified number format function handling sign, decimals, units 2. Always include `+` for positive, `-` for negative 3. Accessibility markup for screenreader clarity ## Proposed fix - Export `fmtChangePercent(pct, opts?)` that always includes sign - Use `{display}` for accessibility - Standardize decimal places (2 for prices, 1 for %, 0 for large numbers) ## Impact Difficult to distinguish direction at a glance; blind users miss numeric context.

title: Sector rotation YTD calculation edge case: off-by-one on year boundary
body:
## Problem
The SectorRotation YTD calculation (SectorRotation.tsx:41-49) iterates through bars and finds the first bar >= Jan 1 of current year. However, it doesn't account for weekends or holidays: if Jan 1 falls on a weekend and trading starts Jan 2, the function counts Jan 2 as "start of year" but an analyst expects Jan 1. Additionally, if historical data is backfilled from a future session (e.g., calculating at 3:59 PM ET), the 'year' could be interpreted differently in different timezones.

## Evidence
- SectorRotation.tsx:42-46: `new Date(bars[i].time * 1000).getFullYear() === year` uses local time
- SectorRotation.tsx:48: Returns `bars.length - i` which assumes contiguous data
- SectorRotation.tsx:69: `w.bars === -1 ? ytdBars(bars)` doesn't validate bars are trading days

## Expected
1. YTD date calculation uses trading calendar (skips weekends/holidays)
2. Timezone-aware comparison (always UTC or always US/ET)
3. Validates input bars for data integrity

## Proposed fix
- Import trading calendar library (e.g., `trading-hours`) for accurate YTD start
- Parse `bars[i].time` as UTC timestamp, not local
- Add data validation: check for gaps, alert if >3 days missing

## Impact
YTD returns (e.g., +8.2% vs. +8.5%) may differ from external tools; audit discrepancies.

title: WatchlistEntrySignals truncates to 10 rows without pagination
body:
## Problem
WatchlistEntrySignals renders only the top 10 tickers (WatchlistEntrySignals.tsx:174: `.slice(0, 10)`) with no indicator that more exist. Users with 50+ watchlist items get no way to see lower-scoring signals, and the truncation happens silently. Additionally, each ticker triggers 3 concurrent react-query requests (quote, historical, fundamentals), which could cause 150+ requests for a 50-ticker list.

## Evidence
- WatchlistEntrySignals.tsx:174: Hard-coded `.slice(0, 10)` with no UI hint
- WatchlistEntrySignals.tsx:27-47: Three separate `useQueries` blocks, each fetches all tickers
- WatchlistEntrySignals.tsx:56: Loading state shown in row but not in parent widget
- No request batching or query concatenation

## Expected
1. Show all tickers or paginate with "show more" button
2. Batch queries to reduce concurrent requests
3. Show loading/error state for entire widget
4. Cache historical/fundamentals across sessions

## Proposed fix
- Add `showAll` state and pagination (10 per page)
- Combine three `useQueries` into one batched query
- Lift loading state to parent component
- Use react-query's `gcTime` to persist data across navigation

## Impact
Users can't analyze full watchlist; unnecessary API spam causes rate-limit hits.

title: Fear & Greed computation uses 20-day SMA on 2-month data
body:
## Problem
The FearGreedPanel fetches SPY 1-year bars, HYG 2-month bars, and TLT 2-month bars (FearGreedPanel.tsx:8-10), then computes 20-day change (chg20 function at line 19-27). However, the function assumes 20 trading days but doesn't validate: if HYG or TLT only has 15 bars due to missing data or short history, `vals[vals.length - 21]` returns undefined and the math fails silently. Additionally, "20-day" hardcoded in the function name contradicts the 2-month query window for HYG/TLT.

## Evidence
- FearGreedPanel.tsx:19-27: `chg20()` doesn't validate `vals.length >= 21`
- FearGreedPanel.tsx:8-10: Fetch windows mismatch (1y for SPY, 2mo for bonds)
- FearGreedPanel.tsx:23-25: `vals[vals.length - 21]` can be undefined
- FearGreedPanel.tsx:29-37: `computeFearGreed()` called with potentially null values but no null-check

## Expected
1. Validate bar count before indexing (>= 21 for 20-day SMA)
2. Consistent history window across all series (e.g., all 3-month)
3. Explicit error handling if data insufficient

## Proposed fix
- Add guard: `if (vals.length < 21) return null` in chg20
- Fetch all three series with same window (3-month)
- Log warning if data < 20 bars
- Show "insufficient data" message if components null

## Impact
Fear/Greed score NaN when bonds missing data; users see broken gauge during market stress.

title: TopMovers returns null when watchlist empty, breaks layout
body:
## Problem
TopMovers component returns `null` when watchlist is empty (TopMovers.tsx:29), which leaves a gap in the grid layout. This is different from other widgets which render a "no data" message. Additionally, users can't distinguish between "no watchlist items" and "watchlist items but no movers today." The component also silently filters items that don't have quote data or price null, without informing the user.

## Evidence
- TopMovers.tsx:9-29: Early return of `null` if `items.length === 0`
- TopMovers.tsx:23: Filters `.filter((r): r is {...}) => r.pct != null` silently
- TopMovers.tsx:24-25: Sorts gainers/losers but no logic if both are empty after filter
- FinanceHome.tsx:122-131: No null-check before rendering TopMovers, assumes component renders

## Expected
1. Render empty state instead of null
2. Show count of filtered-out items (no price data)
3. Distinguish "watchlist empty" from "no movers"

## Proposed fix
- Return empty div with message instead of null
- Add filter count badge
- Move null return into a conditional render at FinanceHome level
- Cache filter results to show in empty state

## Impact
Dashboard layout shifts when watchlist empty, confusing users.

title: Yield curve inverts comparison logic: 5Y > 10Y vs. real-world 2Y
body:
## Problem
The YieldCurve component compares 5Y vs. 10Y yields to detect inversion (YieldCurve.tsx:25-28), but the real-world recession predictor is 2Y > 10Y (2-year inverts before 10-year in a downturn). Using 5Y creates a lagging indicator. Additionally, the code doesn't fetch 2Y data (CURVE only has 13W, 5Y, 10Y, 30Y), making it impossible to fix without refactoring the query.

## Evidence
- YieldCurve.tsx:6-11: CURVE array lacks 2Y ticker
- YieldCurve.tsx:25-28: `short > long` compares 5Y > 10Y, not 2Y > 10Y
- YieldCurve.tsx:19-22: Parsing ambiguity: comments say "×10" but code checks `> 20`
- YieldCurve.tsx:78-79: Title says "US Treasury" but yields may not update in real-time

## Expected
1. Add 2Y yield (^TYX or ^FVX mapped differently)
2. Change inversion logic to 2Y > 10Y
3. Document yield parsing rules
4. Clarify real-time vs. EOD data

## Proposed fix
- Add `{ ticker: '^TYX', label: '2Y', months: 24 }` to CURVE
- Update comparison: `short > long` where short = 2Y
- Add comment explaining Yahoo quote format (×10 for some, direct % for others)
- Add data age indicator

## Impact
Users miss true recession warnings; lagging 5Y > 10Y inversion provides false confidence.
